incidents.title: Synthetic worker lane routing missing in host loop

summary.symptom: Synthetic jarvis-worker lanes were not reachable from the host loop, so worker probes died before any valid worker lifecycle completed.
summary.suspected_cause: The branch had lost the internal host routing path for jarvis-worker-*@nanoclaw and still carried stale worker-run/tooling assumptions from older schemas and E2E expectations.
summary.impact: Worker delegation and Andy status flows were blocked or misreported until the internal worker ingress path, stale-run recovery path, and Andy smoke harness were aligned with the live runtime.
summary.next_action: Monitor credential-proxy stability separately if TLS upstream errors recur, but synthetic worker routing itself is restored and the required gates are passing.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: reported: Worker connectivity probe reaches andy-developer but fails before any worker_runs row is created.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: 2026-04-11 progress: restored registered_groups rows for andy-developer plus synthetic jarvis-worker-1@nanoclaw and jarvis-worker-2@nanoclaw, confirmed service restart with groupCount=4 and OneCLI agent ensure for all three non-main lanes. Added container-runner Apple Container rewrites for OneCLI proxy hosts and staged CA file directory mounts; targeted tests now pass (container-runner, container-runtime, ipc-auth). Remaining live blockers: verify-worker-connectivity still fails because synthetic worker JIDs do not have an internal host ingress/runtime path; host throws No channel for JID for jarvis-worker-* and no worker_runs rows are created. Andy user E2E still times out: host sees @Andy hi and spawns nanoclaw-andy-developer, but no bot reply is produced within SLA.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: 2026-04-11 follow-up: enforced oauth-only launch env for agent containers by stripping OneCLI's injected ANTHROPIC_API_KEY placeholder, added host credential-proxy bypass handling, and set the same bypass inside container/agent-runner before SDK startup. Verification: npm run typecheck passed; src/container-runner.test.ts now covers oauth-only auth env and no-proxy handling. Live result improved materially: a fresh Andy container streamed assistant output and NanoClaw sent a real reply back to 120363407693898924@g.us ('Andy: Hey! How can I help you today?'). Remaining gaps: verify-worker-connectivity is still blocked by missing synthetic worker ingress/runtime, and the DB-based scripts/test-andy-user-e2e.ts probe is still timing out even though the bot reply is now being sent and later appears in the messages table.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: 2026-04-11 resolution: restored synthetic worker ingress in src/index.ts so jarvis-worker-*@nanoclaw dispatches create and complete worker_runs internally, and closed worker containers immediately after a valid completion contract arrives. verify-worker-connectivity now passes end-to-end with fresh review_requested probe runs for jarvis-worker-1 and jarvis-worker-2. Also fixed the stale-run recovery script to match the current worker_runs schema, reconciled the orphaned pre-fix probe row, and updated the Andy DB smoke harness to use deterministic local-bot rows plus realistic live greeting/status expectations. Verification: bash scripts/jarvis-ops.sh verify-worker-connectivity --skip-prechecks => PASS; bash scripts/with-service-node.sh npx tsx scripts/test-andy-user-e2e.ts => PASS.

incidents.title: Main WhatsApp lane container auth bridge missing

summary.symptom: Main WhatsApp lane replied to status probes with login/auth failures instead of concrete control-plane status.
summary.suspected_cause: Container runtime was missing the credential-proxy startup and Anthropic proxy env injection path; earlier in the same outage broken stag...
summary.impact: Main lane could ingest WhatsApp messages but could not reliably execute the container agent end to end, so Andy status queries failed from the user point of view.
summary.next_action: Keep incident open until user confirms the repaired main lane is stable in normal use; watch for any recurrence of container auth or startup errors.

notes.author: operator
notes.text: reported: Main WhatsApp lane replied to status probes with login/auth failures instead of concrete control-plane status.

notes.author: operator
notes.text: Applied fixes in src/container-runner.ts, src/container-runtime.ts, src/index.ts, src/config.ts, and new src/credential-proxy.ts. Verified build passes, targeted src/container-runner.test.ts passes, launchd service runs on Node v20.19.2, and bash scripts/with-service-node.sh npx tsx scripts/test-main-lane-status-e2e.ts now passes both scenarios with concrete Andy status output.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: 2026-04-08 verification: com.nanoclaw launchd service is running and main WhatsApp lane status E2E passed via scripts/test-main-lane-status-e2e.ts, but andy-developer remains offline. Evidence: container ls shows only nanoclaw-agent main container, runtime_owners table is empty, verify-worker-connectivity fails at preflight on missing runtime owner row and missing worker_runs columns run_generation/stop_reason, and logs/nanoclaw.error.log shows OneCLI gateway not reachable so container has no credentials.

incidents.title: NanoClaw start blocked by missing env/auth in active checkout

summary.symptom: NanoClaw start blocked by missing env/auth in active checkout

notes.author: user
notes.text: reported: NanoClaw start blocked by missing env/auth in active checkout

notes.author: operator
notes.text: Resolved startup in active checkout: local .env restored, WhatsApp re-authenticated, better-sqlite3 rebuilt against Node 20 because npm ignore-scripts=true suppressed native addon build, Apple Container default kernel installed via container system start, main chat registered to 919960438648@s.whatsapp.net, launchd com.nanoclaw now running with pid 1519 and WA connected. Follow-up bugs: setup groups helper fails with invalid node --input-type eval quoting; setup verify misreports launchd service as stopped while preflight confirms running.

notes.author: operator
notes.text: Follow-up runtime fix: session cleanup hook was wired to src/session-cleanup.ts but scripts/cleanup-sessions.sh does not exist in this checkout or cached worktrees. Changed startup to skip scheduling cleanup when the hook is absent, log a debug-only notice once, and added regression test src/session-cleanup.test.ts so service startup no longer emits recurring 'Session cleanup failed' errors.

incidents.title: Update workflow blocked in active checkout

summary.symptom: Upstream update could not complete verification in the active checkout without local fixes
summary.suspected_cause: skills-engine snapshot and backup helpers used copyFileSync on symlinked tracked paths; npm ignore-scripts=true left better-sqlite3 on th...
summary.impact: Update apply initially failed, then post-update verification failed until local tooling and config were repaired
summary.next_action: Keep symlink-safe copy helpers and stale-permission pruning in the checkout; document native addon rebuild expectation when ignore-scripts=true

notes.author: operator
notes.text: reported: Upstream update could not complete verification in the active checkout without local fixes

notes.author: operator
notes.text: Local mitigation applied in working tree: skills-engine symlink-safe copy helpers added, better-sqlite3 rebuilt against Node ABI 131 via node-gyp, stale permissions.allow entry for ./container/worker/build.sh removed, npm run build passed, npm test passed (389/389).
